changes.text: Address entry is clearer: the "Look up" button is now "Validate", sits beside "Pin on map", and grays out once your address is confirmed (the app usually validates it for you, so the button mainly matters when you type an address yourself).

On Pin on map, when your pin sits away from the matched address, the "add the exact pin location to the report" option is now an obvious, tappable toggle (with a checkbox) instead of looking like plain text.
Tapping a report type in the category search now selects it on the first tap — previously, with the keyboard open, the first tap only dismissed the keyboard.

Better address detection: when a photo's location, "use my location", or a dropped pin lands mid-block (where DC's address lookup returns only a ZIP code), the app now falls back to your phone's built-in geocoder and re-checks that street address against DC's records — so you get a usable address far more often instead of a "no street address" dead-end. On the map, the live address shown as you move the pin now reflects this too, so it matches what you'll get when you confirm.

Scan a vehicle ID with your camera (experimental): when reporting a dockless vehicle, tap Scan ID with camera to read the printed ID with on-device OCR instead of typing it. It captures automatically once it locks onto the ID, and in low light it turns on a dim camera light by itself (iOS) so you can read the label at night. Everything stays on your device. Marked Experimental while we refine it.

Nearby reports map now groups busy areas into numbered clusters when you're zoomed out, instead of quietly showing only some of the reports. Tap a cluster (or zoom in) to break it into individual report markers — so nothing is hidden when a lot is happening nearby, and the map stays smooth on large screens.
A report you just submitted now shows up in "My reports" right away, instead of sometimes not appearing until a later refresh.
You can now pull to refresh "My reports" from anywhere in the list, not only by pulling down directly on a report.
Opening a report straight from a notification or a shared link and tapping Back now returns you to the home screen instead of doing nothing.
The Nearby reports map now shows when its data was last refreshed (e.g. "last updated 8m ago"), based on when the map data was last rebuilt, making it clearer how recent the reports on screen are.

The Nearby reports map now refreshes its data on its own once it's more than an hour old (the data is rebuilt hourly), instead of only when you restart the app — so leaving the screen open no longer leaves you looking at stale reports.
"Your info" now asks for a first name (alongside a last name and an email or phone) — DC 311 requires it, so some reports were failing to submit without it.

"Your info" now offers DC 311 sign-in to fill your contact details for you — if sign-in is available, the screen leads with a "Sign in to DC 311" option that pulls your name/email/phone from your account, so you don't have to type them (you can still enter them manually).

After you sign in to DC 311, your past reports now reliably appear in "My reports" — the list pulls your account history when you open it and on pull-to-refresh, instead of sometimes showing nothing until much later.

Nearby reports map now groups busy areas into numbered clusters when you're zoomed out, instead of quietly showing only some of the reports. Tap a cluster (or zoom in) to break it into individual report markers — so nothing is hidden when a lot is happening nearby, and the map stays smooth on large screens.
A report you just submitted now shows up in "My reports" right away, instead of sometimes not appearing until a later refresh.
You can now pull to refresh "My reports" from anywhere in the list, not only by pulling down directly on a report.

Report forms no longer show "example" helper entries as if they were selectable answers (one was even pre-selected). The dockless-vehicle "vehicle ID" examples and the bus-stop "Bus Stop Number" example now appear as reference images you can look at, instead of answer options you could accidentally pick or submit.

Roadway Signs report: the "traffic sign" and "parking sign" pickers now show the actual sign choices — with pictures of each sign — instead of a single blank, unselectable option that made those reports impossible to submit.

Report forms now clear follow-up answers that no longer apply when you change an earlier answer: pick one option, then switch to another that hides those follow-ups, and the stale questions (and their now-irrelevant choices) disappear instead of lingering on screen.
The empty "My reports" screen can now be pulled to refresh (previously the "No reports yet" screen ignored the pull gesture).

Achievements & civic points: earn rewards as you report — a bonus the first time you try each category, a milestone every 5 reports, a bump for each day you keep a streak going, and fun one-time trophies (First Report, Power User, streaks, "Rat King" for rodent reports, Night Owl, and more). Rewards pop with a confetti celebration on the confirmation screen, and "Your info" shows your civic-points total, reports, categories, best streak, and trophy collection.

DC 311 sign-in (optional): sign in to your DC 311 account to pull your full report history and DC's responses into the app, and to auto-fill your contact info so you don't have to type it. Entirely optional — you can keep reporting without an account. The home screen shows your sign-in status; if a session expires, your saved contact info is kept and you're prompted to sign in again.

Nearby reports map: a new "Nearby reports" screen (from the home screen) shows open, in-progress, and recently-closed DC 311 requests around you on a map — color- and icon-coded by type, with a ring that fills as a request nears its expected completion date — so you can check whether something you spotted is already reported before filing a duplicate. Tap a pin for details.
Favorite report types: tap the star on any category to pin it to a Favorites list at the top of the home screen for quick access; tap again to remove.

Export / share a report: a report's detail screen can now share a plain-text summary (type, status, dates, location, your description, the agency notice) via the system share sheet — handy for keeping a record or escalating one that was closed but isn't actually fixed.

"Report this issue again": from a report's detail screen, start a fresh report pre-filled from it — same category and location, with a note that references the original ("previous request 26-XXXX … but the issue isn't resolved"), so you don't retype it.
"New" badge in "My reports" marks any report with activity (a status change or a new response) since you last opened it; it clears when you view the report.
The home screen now shows your Favorites instead of an auto-generated "recent reports" list, so it loads instantly with no network wait.
Settings moved to a gear icon in the top-left of the home screen (freeing the spot it used for the new "Nearby reports" entry).

"My reports" now opens instantly — it shows your last-loaded reports right away and refreshes their status in the background, instead of waiting on the network each time. Pull down to refresh on demand.
"My reports" now lists reports newest-first (by latest activity), including the archived section, instead of an inconsistent order.
Settings → "See what's new" opens an in-app changelog: what's included in the version on your device, and what's waiting in your next update.

A report that got stuck because it included an answer to a follow-up question that no longer applied now fixes itself and sends on its own — the app cleans up the saved report against the current questions before retrying, instead of leaving it failed. (Previously you'd have had to re-create it.)
Thank you to our beta testers — your reports are what surfaced the fixes below. Please keep them coming.

Report types with conditional questions (where one answer reveals follow-up questions — e.g. "Roadway marking / striping") now work. The form shows only the questions that apply to your answers and sends only those, so DC 311 no longer rejects the report for answering a question that didn't apply.
When DC 311 can't accept a report for a reason you need to fix (not just a temporary connection issue), the app now tells you why instead of quietly saving it and retrying in the background forever.

If error reporting is turned off, you can now open a specific error in Diagnostics, review exactly what it contains, and choose to send just that one to the developer. The event list marks each report Sent or Not sent at a glance, and a sent report is never sent twice. (With error reporting on, errors send automatically, so there's nothing to do.)

The Settings version footer is now plainly informational — it just shows your app version, build, and current update bundle (tap to copy if support ever asks), instead of the more clinical "report this id" framing.

Errors and warnings now stay in the Diagnostics log instead of being pushed out by routine activity (the log used to drop the oldest events regardless of importance), so you can still find — and send — them later.

Opening an entry in the Diagnostics event log no longer makes the app sluggish. It now opens in a scrollable sheet, and error entries no longer balloon in size internally (their breadcrumb context was nesting and compounding).

A banner now appears at the top of the app whenever you have a report that hasn't been sent to DC 311 yet (queued, sending, or failed) — so a report saved for later can't quietly slip your mind. Tap it to open "My reports" and see status, retry, or finish anything that's missing info.
"Pin on map" now has a center-on-my-location button — tap it to jump the map to where you are instead of panning by hand.

The Settings footer now leads with a bundle id you can tap to copy — the exact id of the code you're running (it changes with each over-the-air update, unlike the build number). Reporting it tells us precisely which version you're on, and it matches the ids in this changelog so you can see what's included. The app version and build number are still shown, as secondary detail.

Reports can no longer slip through with incomplete contact info and then fail to send. The app now reliably requires a last name plus an email or phone before submitting (previously a stray space in one of those fields could pass the check), and a saved report that's still missing that info is now flagged for you to fix in "Your info" instead of being retried against DC 311, which rejects it.

Taking a photo first and then choosing a category now behaves the same as choosing a category first: if your location is missing, outside DC, or has no street address, you get the same heads-up to enter the address — previously the camera-first flow stayed silent and could leave the address blank.

Android: opening "Pin on map" no longer crashes the app. Earlier Android builds shipped without the Google Maps key, so tapping into the map closed the app instantly; the map now loads normally. (iOS was unaffected.)

Diagnostics screen: the toggle is now labeled Error reports (more honest — that's all that's actually sent), and the description no longer mentions the underlying service. The footer's GitHub link has been removed for now.

"Pick the spot" on the map now resolves the address live as you move the pin and shows whether the spot is usable: the matched street address (good) or a warning that there's no street address there (just a zipcode), so you know before committing. When the pin is far from its matched address (the issue is off in a park, lot, or alley — a scooter in the woods), you can opt to attach a precise map link to the exact spot — added to the report's location details. If the spot has no street address at all, the app tells you to move the pin onto a building or enter a nearby address, and offers to add the exact coordinates to the location details.

Diagnostics (Settings): the recent-events list now shows a short preview with the app version visible without scrolling past hundreds of rows. "View all events" opens the full log on its own screen, filterable by level (errors / warnings / info / debug).

Photo uploads no longer spin forever when you walk from WiFi onto cellular (or any other connection drop). The app now watches for upload progress and, if nothing has moved for ~20 seconds, gives up on the current attempt — your photo is kept and the upload is automatically retried as soon as your signal comes back. You'll see a banner on the report screen explaining what's happening if the upload is slow or has stalled, and you can submit the report anyway: it'll be saved and the photo will catch up in the background. Captured photos are now also stashed safely the moment you take them, so you won't lose a shot if iOS reclaims its temp cache while you're filling out the form.

Network requests now time out after 15 seconds instead of spinning forever on a flaky connection. Submitting a report, looking up an address, the map's "Use this location," and "My reports" no longer hang indefinitely — and a timed-out submit is saved and retried automatically, never lost.
The Settings/Diagnostics footer now shows the app's real build number instead of "build ?".

A banner now appears when DC 311 can't be reached (their service is down, or you're offline) that's honest about what still works: you can take photos, fill out reports, and tap Submit, and we'll save them and send automatically when the connection's back. It clears itself the moment the service responds.

Report forms now open instantly from a local cache and refresh in the background, instead of waiting on the network each time. You can fill out and queue a report even when the DC 311 backend is slow or down (it sends once it recovers), and if you haven't started filling it in yet, the form quietly updates to the latest version of the questions.
The keyboard no longer covers the text field you're typing in — the form, "Your info," and "Add a past report" screens now scroll the focused field into view when the keyboard appears.
If a report category fails to load (e.g. the DC 311 backend times out with a 504), the report screen now shows a "Retry" instead of spinning forever.

Delete reports from "My reports": swipe a row left, or use "Remove from my reports" on the detail screen. Removing a filed report only clears it from your list (the 311 request is unaffected); discarding an unsent report stops it from being filed.

Tap any report (in "My reports" or the clipboard preview) to open a full detail screen: status and the complete timeline/SLA dates, the report description, attached photos (tap to open full size), the agency's service notice, and location with ward/ANC and an "Open in Maps" button. Tap the request ID to copy it to the clipboard.
"Add a past report" now detects a service request ID on your clipboard, previews that report (type, status, address), and lets you add it with a single tap instead of typing the ID.

"My reports" now shows more status detail for each filed request: a clear status label, the handling department, and the expected completion date (or a "past expected date" flag, or the completion date once it's done).

Attach multiple photos to a report. The report screen shows a thumbnail strip; add from the camera one at a time or pick several at once from your library (up to 5). Each photo has a remove button, and photos upload in the background so you're never left waiting.
Long service descriptions now have a Show more / Show less toggle, expand to a larger, easier-to-read size, and any links in them are tappable.

Picking the wrong category after snapping a photo no longer loses the photo. You can change the category from the report screen (or with Back) and your photo and location carry over — no need to re-shoot.

Submitting a report without a photo is allowed (photos are optional). The Submit button no longer implies a photo is required; it now tells you what's actually missing (an address, or your contact info).

"My reports" now shows active reports by default — open ones plus anything closed in the last 7 days — and tucks older closed/canceled reports behind a "Show archived" toggle so the list stays manageable over time.
The "Your info" screen makes it clear you only need to fill it in once — your contact details are saved on your device and reused automatically for future reports.
The Diagnostics screen no longer displays the raw Sentry DSN.
